title: Cannot edit insurance form

replies.author: Penguin8R
replies.content:
Check your apache site config, and make sure you have a rewrite rule in place for the APIs directory, it should look something like the following: <Directory /var/www/html/openemr/apis

AllowOverride all

Require all granted

</Directory> When you click on the Insurance Edit button, it’s actually making an API call, and if the server blocks that call, then the Insurance dialog will never load.

replies.author: eumetaxas
replies.content: Hi, when i add these lines the server fails to start File: apache2.conf located in /etc/apache2/ thank you

replies.author: Penguin8R
replies.content: Those lines need to go in your site config, not the apache2.conf file. Those are site specific directives. Note that “/var/www/html/openemr/apis”, is just an example, you’ll need to adjust that to match whatever the web root of your openemr site actually is.

replies.author: eumetaxas
replies.content: Thank you again for coming back. if i add these lines ”<Directory /var/www/html/openemr/apis AllowOverride all Require all granted ” in this file /var/www/html/openemr/sites/default/config.php i cannot login thank you

replies.author: eumetaxas
replies.content:
Grok got me a little bit further but still not working. It fails somewhere here ”Then proceed with the previous steps: ensure certs dir, trigger regen via admin → Globals → Connectors (save) or add/register API client. Check files: text ls -l /var/www/html/openemr/sites/default/documents/certificates/ ” Edit Apache config: sudo nano /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf Add inside <VirtualHost *:80> before </VirtualHost> : <Directory /var/www/html/openemr/apis>
 AllowOverride all
 Require all granted
</Directory> Save (Ctrl+O, Enter, Ctrl+X). Enable rewrite: sudo a2enmod rewrite
sudo systemctl restart apache2 Create certificates dir: sudo mkdir -p /var/www/html/openemr/sites/default/documents/certificates
sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html/openemr/sites/default/documents/certificates Remove rogue API dirs if exist (check first): ls /var/www/html/openemr/apis/default/api/patient/ If shows files/dirs, then: sudo rm -rf /var/www/html/openemr/apis/default/api/patient/* Test insurance edit. If 404/500 persists, reply with browser console errors (F12 > Console). Screenshot from 2026-03-10 09-10-23 1274×454 22 KB

replies.author: Penguin8R
replies.content: Do you access your OpenEMR instance via HTTP or HTTPS? The instructions you reference only show editing the site config for the default web host on port 80. Using OpenEMR via HTTP rather than HTTPS is usually not a good idea. If you are accessing via HTTPS, then your site config for port 443 needs to be updated as well with the directives involving the “apis” directory.

replies.author: eumetaxas
replies.content: Thank you for your time to look at it. via http. Local Ubuntu server. I did not get any further as i am not a programmer and the feature is not essential thankfully. Thank you again

replies.author: Mttx
replies.content: Has anyone find the solution to this?
